executive_summary: PIVOT recommended at 68.7/100 — the pain is real (Problem 75) and unit economics are viable (BizModel 72, LTV/CAC 6.81x), but the market is dominated by SmartBear ($1.5B+) and Postman (30M+ users acquiring Fern), competitive opportunity scored only 4/20, and SOM is a modest $7.5M. Narrowing the niche to a TypeScript-to-OpenAPI CI/CD compiler or targeting Stoplight migration refugees could push this into GO territory.
score_reasoning:
Weighted Score: 68.7/100
Verdict: PIVOT

Score Breakdown:
- Problem: 75/100 × 0.30 = 22.5
- Market: 64/100 × 0.25 = 16.0
- Competitive: 60/100 × 0.15 = 9.0
- BizModel: 72/100 × 0.20 = 14.4
- Risk: 68/100 × 0.10 = 6.8

Knockouts: None
GO Conditions: weighted_score 68.7 < 70 (NOT MET) | Problem 75 >= 60 (met) | All others >= 45 (met)
Confidence: medium

data.executive_summary: PIVOT recommended at 68.7/100 — the pain is real (Problem 75) and unit economics are viable (BizModel 72, LTV/CAC 6.81x), but the market is dominated by SmartBear ($1.5B+) and Postman (30M+ users acquiring Fern), competitive opportunity scored only 4/20, and SOM is a modest $7.5M.
key_strengths: Strong problem validation (75/100): 25+ complaint threads, 8 distinct workarounds, 12 paid alternatives proving willingness to pay
Excellent unit economics (LTV/CAC 6.81x, payback 4.2 months): All three sensitivity scenarios remain viable
Strong revenue model precedent: 6 funded/profitable companies validate freemium/subscription for developer documentation
High execution feasibility (21/25): TypeScript Compiler API public, ts-morph available, low infrastructure costs
Favorable market timing (16/25): 5+ new entrants, 3+ funding rounds, 11.2% CAGR
key_concerns: Dominant incumbent entrenchment (Competitive Opportunity 4/20): SmartBear 1000+ employees, Postman 30M+ users
Modest serviceable market (SOM $7.5M): May not support venture-scale returns without expanding scope
Market consolidation risk: Postman-Fern and SmartBear-Stoplight acquisitions signal platforms absorbing point solutions
Pricing power constrained (13/25): 3+ dominant free alternatives compress willingness to pay
npm supply chain security deterioration: Attacks doubled in 2025, CISA alert issued
critical_assumptions: ARPU $149/mo assumes average customer on team plan (5% conversion rate)
Churn 3.5% monthly is a blend of benchmarks
SOM $7.5M derived from top-down TAM filtering

pivot_suggestions.direction: Focus exclusively on TypeScript-to-OpenAPI CI/CD pipeline automation — become the 'TypeScript compiler for API specs' that feeds into existing doc platforms
pivot_suggestions.addresses: Competitive Opportunity (4/20) — avoids competing with dominant platforms on docs hosting
pivot_suggestions.revalidation_idea: A CLI tool that compiles TypeScript codebases into OpenAPI specs automatically, integrating with existing documentation platforms as a CI/CD pipeline component

pivot_suggestions.direction: Target the post-acquisition Stoplight migration wave — build a migration tool + docs platform for displaced teams
pivot_suggestions.addresses: Market Gap Evidence and Failure Intelligence — captures concrete, time-bound demand
pivot_suggestions.revalidation_idea: A CLI-first API documentation platform with a one-click Stoplight migration tool, targeting displaced teams at $49-149/mo

pivot_suggestions.direction: Pivot to internal/private API documentation for mid-size engineering teams
pivot_suggestions.addresses: Market Scale (SOM 11/25) — internal docs is a larger, less competitive segment
pivot_suggestions.revalidation_idea: An internal API documentation tool for TypeScript monorepos that automatically generates team-facing API contracts

next_steps.action: Run 10 problem interviews with engineering leads at TypeScript-heavy mid-size companies
next_steps.rationale: Need to confirm TypeScript-native differentiation is a real purchasing driver

next_steps.action: Build a landing page for the CI/CD pipeline pivot and measure signup interest
next_steps.rationale: Tests most promising pivot direction with minimal effort

next_steps.action: Monitor Postman-Fern integration roadmap for TypeScript-native features

validation_experiments.experiment: Landing page test on r/node and r/typescript with email waitlist
validation_experiments.what_it_validates: Demand signal for TypeScript-native doc generation

validation_experiments.success_metric: 7/10 confirm they would evaluate a TypeScript-native doc tool
validation_experiments.what_it_validates: Whether TypeScript-native is a real differentiator

validation_experiments.experiment: Prototype TypeScript-to-OpenAPI CLI compiler as open-source
validation_experiments.success_metric: 500+ GitHub stars and 1000+ npm weekly downloads in 30 days
validation_experiments.what_it_validates: Technical feasibility and developer adoption velocity
